behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive/subdirectory event delivery), which often differs by OS and watcher backend. A mocked emitter can force subdirectory events and miss real misconfiguration/backend limits (MOCK-HIDE). Not LLM-dependent, single non-agentic flow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory and write a file there, then assert exactly one pipeline run and output artifact for that nested path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily local dedup state/time-window logic at one object/function boundary; no real OS/network/subprocess is required. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is specifically about 'deduplication logic' and a configurable window, which can be validated with an injected clock and in-memory state.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component twice with the same path and controlled timestamps (inside and outside window) and assert accept/reject outcomes exactly match config.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate events (REAL-INFRA). Mocking event streams can hide real duplicate patterns/coalescing semantics, so System is the minimum to catch real failure.
behaviors.test_description: With the daemon running on a real watched directory, perform writes known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename/edit-save style) and assert one processing invocation/output per logical file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Requires real directory deletion/recreation and watcher re-subscription behavior from OS/filesystem. In-process fakes typically cannot reproduce backend invalidation/rebind edge cases, so mocking would risk false pass.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp watch path, remove that directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff/order orchestration (watch intake -> metadata lookup -> scheduling queue). No mandatory real OS primitive is required to validate ordering policy if timestamps are injected/controlled in-process. Rejected System: behavior text constrains ordering rule but does not require validating OS-specific timestamp/event quirks.
behaviors.test_description: Wire watcher intake, metadata provider, and scheduler in-process; inject simultaneous arrivals with controlled creation timestamps and assert processing order matches timestamp order end-to-end.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real filesystem write lifecycle/visibility timing and event timing from OS. Mocked files/events often miss partial-write races, so MOCK-HIDE applies; System is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real watched directory, start a slow/streaming write to a file, fire/observe arrival during write, assert daemon defers processing until writer closes and file is complete, then processes once.
